title: Isolation of Fidaxomicin and Shunt Metabolites from Actinoplanes deccanensis
author: Jung, Erik
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction:
ZIP File: 
 Characterisation data (such as e.g. NMR, IR, MS spectra) 
 NMR raw data, .mnova files 
 HPLC Raw Data 
 PDF file: 
 Supporting information for 
 Isolation of Fidaxomicin and Shunt Metabolites from Actinoplanes deccanensis 
   
 Abstract of the corresponding publication: 
 A protocol for the isolation of the antibiotic fidaxomicin (Fdx) from  Actinoplanes deccanensis  and the isolation of shunt metabolites from  A. deccanensis fdxG2-  is reported. We constructed the mutant strain  A. deccanensis fdxG2-  by genetic manipulation which enabled the isolation of shunt metabolites as useful starting points for semisynthetic analogues of Fdx. Furthermore, a synthetic protocol for the conversion of complex  A. deccanensis fdxG2-  extracts into the single compound FdxG2-OH via methanolysis is presented. This synthetic procedure is complemented by images and practical notes. Full structure assignment is given in the SI and the characterization data files are published to aid experimentalists. The protocol is also suitable as an undergraduate laboratory project. We hope to facilitate research into new Fdx derivatives through the availability of this procedure.  Natural Products, Antibiotics, Metabolic Engineering, Natural Product Isolation, Chemical Education
